w:r.w:t: , prepared and submitted by Achas, John Russell E., Bañez, Brenda Eunice O., Cañada, Charlie C., Millan, Klint Rylee C., Paalisbo, Ivan James E., and Paquibulan, CJ Mark T. in partial fulfillment of the requirements for the strand
, has been examined and recommended for
Accepted in partial fulfillment of the requirements for the strand
Unique Selling Proposition and Value Proposition
5.1 Partnership Agreement/ Company Bylaws and other legal requirements documents.
5.2 Description of the logo and other promotional paraphernalia.
5.3 Survey and other related market research.
5.4 Management Schedule and other management activities.

TunaFlow is a pioneering technology company dedicated to modernizing the global seafood industry through artificial intelligence and advanced digital infrastructure. Headquartered in a high-growth tech hub, TunaFlow operates as a B2B Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) provider, specializing in the niche but high-value sector of tuna commerce. Our organization is built by a team of software engineers, data scientists, and seafood logistics experts focused on bridging the gap between traditional fishing practices and the digital economy.

The flagship TunaFlow platform is an AI-driven management ecosystem designed specifically for the unique challenges of perishable tuna products. It features real-time inventory tracking, automated Demand Forecasting, and a proprietary Dynamic Pricing Engine. By analyzing historical sales data, local market trends, and product freshness metrics, TunaFlow provides sellers with optimized pricing suggestions that maximize profitability while minimizing the risk of product spoilage. The platform integrates seamlessly with existing warehouse operations, offering a user-friendly dashboard that transforms complex data into clear, actionable business insights.

The global tuna market is a multi-billion dollar industry characterized by high volatility, extreme perishability, and complex supply chains. Currently, many participants rely on manual pricing and legacy inventory systems, leading to significant revenue loss through waste and suboptimal pricing. TunaFlow targets high-volume tuna exporters, processors, and premium retailers who manage high-value inventory. As the demand for transparency and sustainability in seafood grows, TunaFlow is uniquely positioned to capture the market shift toward data-driven, ethical supply chain solutions.

TunaFlow's revenue model is based on a tiered subscription structure for its SaaS platform, complemented by transaction-based fees for marketplace integrations. Based on pilot data and market analysis, we project that early adopters will see a 15% to 30% reduction in waste-related losses and a 10% increase in overall margins within the first six months of implementation. For the organization itself, we anticipate reaching a break-even point in Year 2, with a projected year-over-year revenue growth of 200% as the platform scales to support more seafood categorie
To lead the future of seafood commerce by perfecting the flow of tuna through intelligent pricing and sustainable supply chain innovation.

Our mission is to modernize the tuna industry through high-frequency data analysis and automated pricing engines. TunaFlow Bridges the gap between traditional fishing and modern commerce by offering a seamless digital ecosystem that outpaces legacy systems. We focus daily on perfecting our algorithms and warehouse management tools to ensure that freshness is preserved, waste is eliminated, and market value is always optimized

TunaFlow is built on a foundation of ethical commerce and technological excellence. Our philosophy is rooted in the belief that the seafood industry can be both highly profitable and deeply sustainable through the power of intelligent data.
Precision: We eliminate guesswork by leveraging AI to provide a clear, data-driven path for pricing and inventory decisions.
Sustainability: We are committed to a zero-waste future, ensuring every harvest reaches its destination efficiently to respect our oceans' resources.
Transparency: We build trust among all stakeholders
from local fishermen to global retailers
through honest data and clear communication.
Innovation: We remain at the forefront of AI research, constantly evolving our tools to solve the world's most complex seafood logistics challenges.

The name TunaFlow was conceptualized to represent the dynamic and seamless movement of perishable goods in a modern marketplace. "Tuna" identifies our core expertise and the high-value commodity we serve, while "Flow" symbolizes our commitment to frictionless supply chains and the continuous, real-time adjustment of market value.
To lead the future of seafood commerce by perfecting the flow of tuna through intelligent pricing and sustainable supply chain innovation.

TunaFlow empowers seafood businesses by providing advanced, AI-driven tools for real-time inventory management and pricing optimization, ensuring sustainability and profitability are achieved together through everyday operational excellence.
TunaFlow operates as a Technology-Enabled B2B Marketplace and Logistics

Platform. It is registered as a Private Corporation, allowing for rapid scaling and investment in proprietary AI research. Our primary business function is bridging the gap between seafood producers and commercial buyers through optimized digital infrastructure.
Sample Floor Plan Concept: The TunaFlow headquarters features an "Optimized Workflow" layout:
The AI Command Center: A central hub with floor-to-ceiling screens showing real-time global seafood pricing and inventory flow.
Agile Engineering Pods: Open-plan spaces designed for rapid collaboration between software engineers and supply chain experts.
The Quality Lab: A specialized section for testing product freshness sensors and IoT hardware.
Stakeholder Lounge: A collaborative space for meetings with suppliers and enterprise customers
Manages budgeting, accounting, and financial planning; ensures compliance with financial regulations and monitors company profitability.
Oversees project planning and execution, coordinates between departments, manages timelines, and ensures project goals are met on time and within budget.
Leads the development team, writes and reviews code, sets coding standards, and ensures the software architecture aligns with project needs.
Develops marketing strategies, manages campaigns, promotes the brand, and works to attract and retain customers
Manages the customer support team, handles customer complaints, ensures quality service, and develops support protocols and training.
Leads innovation efforts, conducts product research, tests new ideas, and develops new features or products to stay ahead of market trends.

Our business focuses on the processing, packaging, and distribution of high-quality tuna products sourced directly from local fishermen. The company aims to provide fresh, affordable, and export-quality tuna products while supporting the local fishing industry
Provides fresh and high-quality tuna products for households and restaurants
Reduces spoilage and waste using real-time freshness monitorin
Supports sustainability and responsible seafood commerce
Ensures proper sanitation and food safety standards
The core purpose of TunaFlow is to eliminate guesswork in pricing decisions while ensuring freshness and profitability.
Available in bulk and retail sizes
Food-grade vacuum-sealed plastic packs with labeled expiration dates and storage instructions.
Quality Level: Premium Grade – Carefully selected tuna, inspected for freshness, color, and texture before processing.
Replacement for defective or damaged products
Delivery Service – Direct delivery to restaurants, markets, and supermarkets
Customer Support – Order assistance and bulk pricing inquiries
Our tuna products focus on freshness, safety, and quality to meet the growing demand for seafood in both local and commercial markets.
This flowchart explains the operational process of tuna production:

As a tuna processing and distribution business, we carefully considered the technological requirements necessary to maintain product freshness, ensure food safety, and improve operational efficiency. Since tuna is a highly perishable commodity, the business relies heavily on proper storage, sanitation, and packaging technologies to preserve quality from the time of receiving until final delivery. Although we are not operating at the scale of large corporations such as General Tuna Corporation, we aim to maximize available resources and apply appropriate technologies that meet industry standards while remaining cost-effective for a growing enterprise.

For cold storage and preservation, the business will utilize commercial-grade chillers and industrial freezers to maintain tuna at safe temperature levels. Fresh tuna will be stored at 0°C to 4°C for short-term holding, while frozen products will be maintained at -18°C to preserve texture, taste, and nutritional value. Digital temperature monitoring devices will be installed to ensure consistent temperature control and to reduce the risk of spoilage. Proper cold chain management is essential in seafood processing, especially in tuna-producing areas such as General Santos City, where quality preservation determines competitiveness in the market.

For processing operations, stainless steel preparation tables, food-grade cutting tools, and sanitizing equipment will be used to ensure hygiene and compliance with food safety standards. All processing equipment will be corrosion-resistant and easy to clean to prevent contamination. The company will implement strict cleaning schedules and sanitation protocols to maintain a safe working environment and protect product integrity.

Packaging technology is another important consideration. The business will use vacuum sealing machines to remove air from packaging, extending shelf life and maintaining freshness. Products will be labeled with production dates, expiration dates, and storage instructions to ensure transparency and customer safety. Vacuum-sealed packaging not only improves preservation but also enhances product presentation and professionalism in the market.

In terms of distribution, insulated delivery vehicles and portable temperature monitoring devices will be utilized to maintain safe storage conditions during transportation. Maintaining the cold chain from processing facility to customer location is critical to preventing spoilage and ensuring customer satisfaction. Proper transportation technology also reduces product loss and increases operational reliability.

To support internal operations, the business plans to implement basic inventory monitoring systems to track stock levels, manage daily sales, and prevent overstocking or understocking. As the business expands, more advanced inventory management software may be integrated to improve efficiency and data accuracy.

For marketing and customer engagement, the company will use social media platforms and digital communication tools to receive orders, promote products, and connect with customers. Online ordering and digital payment options will improve convenience and expand market reach beyond traditional sales channels.
It enumerates all primary start-up needs of the business
A business workflow is a repeatable process consisting of tasks that
be completed in a specific sequence. Think of it as work flowing from one stage to the next until it is finished.

The global tuna and seafood industry is a multi-billion-dollar market with strong international demand. However, many businesses still rely on manual inventory systems and traditional pricing strategies, leading to waste and financial losses. With the rapid digital transformation of supply chains worldwide, businesses are increasingly adopting AI-based systems to improve operational efficiency and reduce spoilage. TunaFlow addresses this demand by offering specialized pricing and inventory solutions tailored for tuna commerce.
This section refers to the current and future number of customers and the number of units. You can get this information through government data, trade associations, and
refers to the projected increase in the market size. The market growth rate is based on past data, which might be helpful for your sales forecast.
The market growth rate is usually expressed as a percentage and is calculated by comparing the current market size with the size in a previous period. It reflects
a market's overall health and potential
and is used by businesses and investors to assess the attractiveness and profitability of entering or expanding within that market. A higher market growth rate generally indicates greater
, while a lower rate may imply limited potential for expansion.
analyzes past and current market behavior and dominant patterns of the market and consumers.
Understanding market trends is crucial for businesses as it helps them anticipate and adapt to shifting customer demands and industry dynamics. By following market trends, businesses can
identify new opportunities, anticipate potential challenges, and make informed decisions to stay competitive.
gathering and analyzing information about existing and potential customers. It involves studying their characteristics, preferences, behaviors, needs, and buying patterns.
The most common method of customer analysis
s choosing which customer segmentation is appropriate for the business – behavioral, demographic, psychographic, etc.
After choosing one or a combination of the customer segmentation, create a list or table that showcase
the specific characteristics you look for in your target market.
who their competitors are and what they are doing.
The competitive analysis will show the
comparative table for direct and indirect competitors of the company.
Firms often conduct the PESTLE analysis to get a detailed overview of what external factors determine the trends. It also helps to predict what might happen in the future.
is an acronym that stands for

: Political developments can highly influence individuals and organizations. It is essential to be aware of likely upcoming shifts in power. Political developments can affect the environment, antitrust, financial markets, trade, and other laws. Factors include political stability, regulation of monopolies, tax policies, price regulations, consumer protection, jurisdiction, and trade unions.

The economic condition is strongly associated with the consumers' buying position. This step considers factors such as interest rates, international trade, taxes, savings, inflation, subsidies, availability of jobs, and entrepreneurship.
The social developments include consumer behavior, demographics, religion, lifestyles, values, and advertising.

The technology aspect focuses highly on technological advancements. It includes factors like innovation, communication, energy, transport, research and development, patent regulations, and the life cycle of products.
the factors that affect businesses due to, or directly related to, governmental laws
a big part in deciding how companies operate, what profits they receive, and how customers behave.
Environmental developments involve ecosystem factors such as water, wind, food, soil, energy,

Strengths refer to the internal factors within an organization that give it a competitive advantage or contribute to its success. These could include a strong brand reputation, a dedicated and skilled workforce, a superior product or service offering, efficient operations, an extensive distribution network, or unique technological capabilities. By identifying and leveraging their strengths, organizations can position themselves effectively in the market and capitalize on opportunities that align with their core competencies.

Weakness in SWOT analysis refers to internal factors or characteristics of a business or individual that put them at a disadvantage or undermine their overall performance. It represents areas where the entity may lack skills, resources, or competitive advantages, which could impede its ability to achieve its goals. Weaknesses can include inadequate market knowledge, limited financial resources, deficiencies in infrastructure, ineffective marketing strategies, or an absence of technological expertise. Identifying and addressing weaknesses is crucial in SWOT analysis as it enables organizations to develop plans and strategies to mitigate these vulnerabilities and improve overall performance.
Opportunities in SWOT analysis refer to external factors that
can positively impact an organization's performance or strategic objectives
. They are favorable conditions or trends in the external environment that

to its advantage. Identifying and capitalizing on opportunities is crucial for organizations to stay competitive and achieve growth. These opportunities may arise from emerging markets, new technologies, changes in consumer preferences, favorable government policies, strategic partnerships, or other external factors. By recognizing and properly leveraging opportunities, organizations can gain a competitive edge, expand their market share, improve profitability, or achieve other strategic goals.
Threats refer to external factors or challenges that may impede the success or progress of a business or project. They can negatively impact the
performance or competitiveness of an organization. Threats can arise from various sources

such as competitors, changing market trends, new regulations, economic conditions, technological advancements, or even natural disasters. Identifying threats through a SWOT (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats) analysis allows organizations to develop strategies to mitigate or navigate these risks effectively.
Unique Selling Proposition and Value Proposition
uniqueness of the product or service
. The firm must identify why the effect would be attractive to the customers.
Based on the marketing objectives, this firm should decide on a general pricing strategy for the product.
Different pricing schemes for each product are acceptable if the firm has several outcomes
The firm outlines how the brand gets to the customer in this part.
The selected method must be made after considering several factors, such as cost, efficiency, and product typ

In this section, the business must describe its advertising and promotions strategy. They must decide on the advertising and marketing objectives, adopt a plan that fits the target market profile, and decide on the message and media the firm will use. The company must also specify details of the media used, such as time and location, frequency, and associated costs.

Capital expenditure, or CapEx, is funds a company uses to acquire, upgrade, and maintain physical assets such as property, industrial buildings, or equipment. CapEx is often used to undertake new projects or investments by the firm. This type of financial outlay is also made by companies to maintain or increase the scope of their operations.
It describes how much the shares or contributions
of each partner or investor are.
It can be made by individuals, companies, or other entities and is typically used to finance the
business's initial setup, expansion, or ongoing operation

s. Capital contributions can take various forms, such as cash, equipment, property, or intellectual property rights. The amount of capital contributed is recorded on the company's balance sheet as part of its equity or owner's capital. It represents
contributors' ownership interest and financial stak
Operating expenses are the costs for a company to run daily business operations. Examples include
ny expenses considered sales, general, & administrative expenses or SG&A on the income statement
The Income statement, the profit and loss statement, summarizes the company's revenue and expenses. The income statement's bottom line shows the company's net income or revenues minus costs.
The payback period must be computed using the formula provided in the class lesson
Return on Investment (ROI) is a performance measure used to evaluate the efficiency of an investment or compare the efficiency of
particular investment relative to the investment's cost.
